Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

TCL 06, 09 [Šumma Alu 120][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[* gišMA₂.TUŠ].A gišMA₂ dAMAR.[UTU ...]

(o 1) (If) the matušû-boat, Marduk's boat [...]

o 22

[...] ⸢ID₂⸣ is-ḫu-up [...]

(o 2) [...] (in) the river, overturns [...]

o 33

[...] x iš-še-bi-ir DINGIR-MEŠ ana KUR ⸢BI⸣ [...]

(o 3) [...] ... is broken: the gods will [...] to that land.

o 44

[...] iš-še-bi-ir DINGIR-MEŠ ina SU KUR BAD-MEŠ : [...]

(o 4) [...] is broken: the gods will withdraw from the heart of the land (or): [...].

o 55

[...] iš-še-bi-ir DINGIR-MEŠ KUR BI i-sak₆-ki-pu

(o 5) [...] is broken: the gods will overthrow that land.

o 66

[...] ⸢GUB₃⸣-ša₂ iš-še-bi-ir DINGIR-MEŠ KUR BI ir-ra-ru

(o 6) [...] is broken (on) its left: the gods will curse that land.

o 77

[...] ⸢EGIR⸣-šu₂ iš-še-bi-ir DINGIR-MEŠ KUR BI ana ḪUL!ti₃ UŠ-MEŠ

(o 7) [...] is broken (on) its rear: the gods will evilly lead that land.

o 88

[*] KI.MIN<(gišMA₂.TUŠ.A)> gišMA₂ dAMAR.UTU is-sa₃-pi-iḫ DINGIR-MEŠ EN ul-<la> KUR BIR-MEŠ

(o 8) (If) ditto (the matušû-boat, Marduk's boat) is (all) broken up: the gods will scatter the (people of the) land forever.

o 99

[*] KI.MIN<(gišMA₂.TUŠ.A)> gišMA₂ dAMAR.UTU iṭ-bu KUR BI NIG₂.GIG IGI

(o 9) (If) ditto (the matušû-boat, Marduk's boat) sinks: (the people of) that land will experience trouble.

o 1010

* KI.MIN<(gišMA₂.TUŠ.A)> gišMA₂ dAMAR.UTU šu-šu-rat dEN.LIL₂ ana KUR ana SIG₅ti u₂-šar

(o 10) (If) ditto (the matušû-boat, Marduk's boat) is in good condition: Ellil will bow down towards the land with kindness.

o 1111

10 MU-BI.IM ta-mar-ti gišMA₂.TUŠ.A gišMA₂ dAMAR.UTU

(o 11) 10 lines: appearance of the matušû-boat, Marduk's boat.

o 1212

* ANŠE.KUR.RA ša gišGIGIR DINGIR is-kil UMUŠ KUR MANni

(o 12) (If) the horse of the god's chariot rears up: the mind of the land will change.

o 1313

* ANŠE.KUR.RA ša gišGIGIR DINGIR is-kil-ma ḫu-ṣab gišGIGIR iš-bir LU₂ BI ina-ziq

(o 13) (If) the horse of the god's chariot rears up and smashes the chariot (into) twigs: that man should worry.

o 1414

* ANŠE.KUR.RA ša gišGIGIR DINGIR is-kil-ma u₂-gal-lit NIDBA DINGIR BI KU₅as

(o 14) (If) the horse of the god's chariot rears up and frightens (the people): food offerings will cease (for) that god.

o 1515

* ANŠE.KUR.RA ša gišGIGIR DINGIR is-kil-ma LU₂ ik-rit NAM.UŠ₂-MEŠ ina KUR GAL₂ši

(o 15) (If) the horse of the god's chariot rears up and hits a man: there will be plague in the land.

o 1616

* LUGAL ŠU DINGIR DAB-ma lu ina E₃-šu₂ lu ina KU₄-šu₂ is-kil EME ḪULti₃

(o 16) (If) the king seizes the god's hand and gets stuck either when he goes out or when he enters: evil speech will surge against the king; the king will defeat his enemy.

o 1717

KI LUGAL šit-pa-at LUGAL a-a-ab-šu₂ KURad₂

o 1818

* LUGAL gišMA₂.LA₂ SA₅-ma gišMA₂.LA₂ is-si ša₂ LUGAL šu-a-tu

(o 18) (If) the king fills the malallû-boat and the malallû-boat makes a loud noise: concerning that king, his prince will die before his (intended) days.

o 1919

NUN-šu₂ ina la₃ u₄-mi-šu₂ UŠ₂

o 2020

* tu₁₅U₁₈.LU DU-ma si-ir E₂ DINGIR u₂-na-ši-ip E₂.GAR₈ E₂ DINGIR GUL-MEŠ

(o 20) (If) the south wind blows and clears off the temple's wall-plaster; (if) the temple's wall is totally destroyed and everything that belongs to the temple is broken up: a plague will afflict (the land).

o 2121

u₃ mim-mu-u₂ E₂ DINGIR ḪAŠir NAM.UŠ₂-MEŠ GAR-MEŠ

o 2222

* ina ba-lu me₂-ḫe-e lu ša₂-ki-<ru>-ut DINGIR lu NIG₂.NA DINGIR lu gišBANŠUR DINGIR it-ten-šu₂

(o 22) (If) without a storm, either the šakirūtu-equipment of the god, or the god's censer or the god's offering table are always unstable or (if) the god shifts on his seat: the enemy will quickly fall onto the land and the regular offerings will cease.

o 2323

u₂-lu DINGIR ina KI.TUŠ-šu₂ TUKU₄uš KUR₂ ana KUR ar₂-ḫiš ŠUB-ma sat-tuk-ka KU₅as

o 2424

* u₄-um DINGIR! IRI ŠEG₃! SURnun NAM.UŠ₂-MEŠ MU-GAL₂-MEŠ DINGIR IRI KI IRI-šu₂ ze-ni

(o 24) (If on) the day of the city's god, the rain rains: there will be plague; the city's god will be angry with his city.

o 2525

* u₄-um DINGIR IRI dIŠKUR is-si lu₂SA.GAZ SAG.DU KU₅is

(o 25) (If on) the day of the city's god, Adad roars: a bandit will behead (people).

o 2626

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI NA₄ SURnun KUR u₂-sur-tu₂ IGI ana LUGAL šul₃-mu

(o 26) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), hailstone rains: the land will experience a siege; for the king, health.

o 2727

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI me-ḫu-u₂ DUik SU.GU₇ dan-nu ina KUR GAL₂ši

(o 27) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), a storm blows: there will be a severe famine in the land.

o 2828

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI lu₂ḪAL bi-ri u₂-šab-ri DI BI NU SI.SA₂

(o 28) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), a diviner conducts a divination: the lawsuit will not go well.

o 2929

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI KASKAL iṣ-bat AŠ₂-su NU KURad₂

(o 29) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he takes the road: he will not achieve his wish.

o 3030

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI DI NA iDU₁₀.GA!(UB) NAM.SIG IGI

(o 30) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), the man's lawsuit is favourable: he will experience poverty.

o 3131

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI DI NA DU₁₁.DU₁₁ub NIG₂.GIG IGImar

(o 31) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he brings a legal case against a man: he will experience evil.

Reverse
r 1r 1

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI [x (x)] x-ma NAM.ERIM₂ DAB-su

(r 1) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), [...] ... and a curse will afflict him.

r 22

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI [x in?-ne₂?]-ṣe-el ŠA₃.ḪUL UŠ-MEŠ-šu

(r 2) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), [...] he becomes paralysed: sorrow will always pursue him.

r 33

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI ⸢ni⸣-gu-tu₂ GARun ŠA₃.ḪUL₂.LA UŠ-MEŠ-šu?

(r 3) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he performs a joyful song: happiness will always accompany him.

r 44

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI ana IGI DINGIR-šu₂ i-ṣi-iḫ ŠA₃ DINGIR e-ze-es-su

(r 4) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he laughs in front of his god: the god's heart will be angry with him.

r 55

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI ana IGI DINGIR-šu₂ ER₂ DINGIR-šu i-rim-šu

(r 5) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he weeps in front of his god: his god will be merciful to him.

r 66

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI A₂.TUKU ki-mil-ti DINGIR-šu

(r 6) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), (he makes) profit: wrath of his god.

r 77

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI iš-bi nap-lu-us DINGIR-šu

(r 7) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he is satisfied: (favourable) look (from) his god.

r 88

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI šip-ru DU-šu ZIbu kaš-du

(r 8) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), a message comes to him: the attack will be successful.

r 99

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI ana MUNUS TEḫe ki-mil-ti DINGIR GAL₂ši

(r 9) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he approaches a woman: there will be a god's wrath.

r 1010

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI is-si-ma ŠUBut DINGIR-šu₂ KI-šu₂ zi-ir

(r 10) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he shouts and falls: his god will turn against him.

r 1111

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI is-si-ma ana 15 ŠUBut ul DABsu

(r 11) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he shouts and falls to the right: he will not seize him.

r 1212

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI MIN<(is-si-)>ma ana 2.30 ŠUBut <<ana>> MUNUS.ḪUL-šu₂ IGImar

(r 12) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), ditto (he shouts) and falls to the left: he will see his misfortune.

r 1313

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI ana UGU IGI-šu₂ ŠUBut ana MU BI UŠ₂

(r 13) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), he falls on his face: he will die within the year.

r 1414

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI NIG₂.LA₂ ip!(LU-)pu-us-su ZI kaš-du

(r 14) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), a yoked team (of animals) knocks him over: the attack will be successful.

r 1515

* KI.MIN<(u₄-um)> DINGIR! IRI u₂-ma-am ṣa-an-du ip!(LU-)pu-us-su ana IGI DINGIR

(r 15) (If) ditto ((on) the day of the city's god), a yoked animal knocks him over (but) he does not cross his god's path: wrath of his god.

r 1616

la₃ GIB ki-mil-ti DINGIR-šu

r 1717

* u₄-um NA ana TIRUM iš-ku-nu tu₁₅U₁₈.LU DUik SIG₅

(r 17) (If on) the day they appoint a man to the tīru-office, the south wind blows: he will prosper.

r 1818

[*] ⸢MIN⸣<(u₄-um)> NA ana TIRUM iš-ku-nu tu₁₅SI.SA₂ DUik NIG₂.TUK GU₇

(r 18) (If on) the day ditto (they appoint a man to the tīru-office), the north wind blows: he will enjoy riches.

r 1919

[...] ⸢tu₁₅⸣KUR.RA DUik U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ LUGUD₂.DA-MEŠ

(r 19) [...] the east wind blows: his days will be short.

r 2020

[... tu₁₅]MAR.TU DUik NU SIG₅

(r 20) [...] the west wind blows: he will not prosper.

r 2121

[... mul]dele-bat ṣer-ḫa TUKU SIG₅

(r 21) [...] Venus has a flare: he will prosper.

r 2222

[... mul]dele-bat ṣer-ḫa NU TUKU NU SIG₅

(r 22) [...] Venus does not have a flare: he will not prosper.

(colophon)
r 2323

[...] x E GARin x x ZIḫi

(r 23) [...] Excerpted (from) "[(If) ...] ... is located ...".

r 2424

[... mdINANA]—⸢MU—KAM⸣ ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za!-kir lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ

(r 24) [...] Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir, incantation priest.
